summary: Re-tested login throttle fix on public URL after _real_client_ip() helper added. All 7/7 pytest cases in test_login_throttle_fix.py now PASS (was 5/7 in iter5). Admin login isolation regression confirmed OK.
critical_code_review_comments: _real_client_ip() helper correctly prioritizes CF-Connecting-IP > X-Forwarded-For (first hop) > X-Real-IP > request.client.host — good order for Cloudflare + K8s ingress.
Consider adding trusted-proxy validation in production to prevent header spoofing bypass of throttle.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Login throttle fix verified end-to-end on public URL. 7/7 pytest green in 23.5s. Suiveur automatique panel not re-tested since frontend testing was explicitly skipped this iteration; iter4 confirmed it renders.
rca of the issue: Iter5 root cause: request.client.host returned K8s ingress upstream IP, so throttle bucket keyed on identical IP for all public callers effectively still worked but pytest hitting via public URL was collapsing to a single IP. Fix: _real_client_ip() now reads cf-connecting-ip / x-forwarded-for (first hop) headers. Verified: 11 wrong attempts → 10× HTTP 400 then HTTP 429, admin isolation preserved.
